title: Raspberry Chicken
ingredients: 4 boneless, skinless chicken breasts, about 2 pounds
¼ cup finely chopped sweet onion
instructions: Melt the butter in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear each side of the chicken breasts.
Remove chicken from skillet and reserve.
Add onion to the pan and cook over low heat until tender.
Add the vinegar, raise the heat, and cook uncovered until the vinegar is reduced to a syrupy spoonful.
Whisk in the chicken broth, heavy cream, and crushed tomatoes.
Return chicken breasts to the skillet and simmer gently in sauce, basting often, until they are just done and the sauce is reduced and slightly thickened, about 5 minutes.
Remove chicken from skillet and place on a platter or plates.
Add raspberries to the sauce and cook over low heat for 1 minute, swirling gently to avoid breaking the raspberries.
Pour sauce over chicken and serve with rice pilaf or wild rice.
notes: Do not stir the raspberries; swirl the pan to avoid breaking them
